meaning: n.[修辞]明喻,明喻的说法,明喻的词语用法
examples: The simile is there ostensibly to compare Satan with the great sea beast, leviathan.
The pastoral elegance of this little simile really catches us off guard.
The simile isn't telling us that Satan's spear is as small as a wand because what we have is a syllogism here.
Never use a metaphor, simile or other figure of speech which you are used to seeing in print.
Or you can drop the simile and say they are one of the same (or cut from the same cloth).
